hypothesis: If a GraphSAGE-based two-node graph using frozen single-gas features from O2 and Ar augmented with the corresponding Ar fraction as node attributes is trained to predict the ten radial etching rates for Ar/O2 mixtures, then it will achieve higher predictive accuracy than a baseline feedforward network and other GNN architectures across varying coil power and pressure because leveraging transferable single-gas representations and inter-gas relationships improves generalization to mixtures.
